input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

data.content:
Here are the likely causes and fixes:

**1. `GoToAsync("productdetails")` throws an exception**

The route isn't registered. Non-visual routes (not declared in Shell XAML) must be registered explicitly:

```csharp
// In App.xaml.cs or MauiProgram.cs
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(ProductDetailsPage));
```

Then navigate with:
```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails");
```

**2. `GoToAsync("//products/active")` — navigates but nothing visible changes**

The absolute route (`//`) switches the active `ShellContent`, but if your `TabBar`/`Tab` items share the same page type or the `ContentTemplate` isn't set, the visual won't update. Most common cause: **missing `ContentTemplate`** — the page is eagerly created once and reused. Fix:

```xml
<!-- Use DataTemplate so each ShellContent gets its own page instance -->
<Tab Title="Products">
    <ShellContent Route="active"
                  Title="Active"
                  ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:ActiveProductsPage}" />
    <ShellContent Route="archived"
                  Title="Archived"
                  ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
</Tab>
```

Also verify each `ShellContent` has a **distinct page type** or that `OnNavigatedTo`/`OnAppearing` refreshes the UI. If both point to the same page class, the framework may consider it a no-op.

**3. 3-second startup with 12 pages**

Shell eagerly creates pages that use the `Content` property directly. Switch all `ShellContent` items to use **`ContentTemplate`** for deferred (lazy) loading:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Eager — page created at startup -->
<ShellContent Route="settings">
    <local:SettingsPage />
</ShellContent>

<!-- ✅ Lazy — page created on first navigation -->
<ShellContent Route="settings"
              ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:SettingsPage}" />
```

With 12 pages, this alone typically eliminates the bulk of the delay since only the initially visible page is instantiated at launch.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about three common .NET MAUI Shell navigation issues. These are well-known problems with known solutions. Let me provide concise, accurate answers.
